shortDef: Cannot see. −2 to AC, loses Dex bonus to AC, −4 to most Str- and Dex-based skill checks, 50% miss chance on attacks.
Acts randomly each round: attack nearest, do nothing, babble, or attack self.
Frozen in fear. Loses Dex bonus to AC; opponents gain +2 to hit.
Cannot take actions for 1 round. AC not affected.
Impaired by light or visual effects. −1 to attack rolls and Perception.
Hit points fall to −(Con score) or lower, or killed by ability damage/drain. Soul departs body.
Cannot hear. −4 initiative, 20% spell failure for spells with verbal components.
Unconscious and losing hit points. Must succeed on a DC 10 Con check each round or die.
Negative levels reduce all d20 rolls and effective level by 1 each. At 0 effective level, the creature dies.
Movement halved; cannot run or charge. −2 to attack rolls and Dex.
Speed halved; −6 to Str and Dex. Rest 1 hour → becomes fatigued.
Stands and watches something. −4 to skill checks made as reactions. Any threat breaks the effect.
Cannot run or charge; −2 to Str and Dex. Becoming fatigued again → exhausted.
Has not yet acted; loses Dex bonus to AC and cannot make attacks of opportunity (unless has Uncanny Dodge).
Flees from the source of fear if possible. −2 to attack rolls, saves, skill checks, and ability checks.
Restrained by an opponent. −4 to Dex, −2 to attack and combat maneuver checks; cannot move.
Paralyzed, held, bound, sleeping, unconscious, or otherwise unable to act. Dex treated as 0; attackers gain +4 to hit.
Has no physical body. Immune to non-magical attacks; 50% chance to ignore magic. Can pass through solid objects.
Cannot be seen normally. +2 to attack, denies Dex bonus to AC. Attackers have a 50% miss chance.
Can only take a single move action per round; cannot attack, cast spells, concentrate, or do anything else.
Runs from source of fear; −2 to saves and checks. Drops held items randomly. Stronger version of frightened.
Frozen in place; Str and Dex treated as 0. Cannot act; attackers gain +4 bonus and can deliver coup de grace.
Turned to stone; considered unconscious. Hardness 8 and natural AC +6.
Held immobile while grappling. −4 Dex, loses Dex to AC, can speak but not cast spells with somatic components.
Lying on the ground. −4 to melee attacks; +4 AC against ranged, −4 AC against melee.
Mild fear. −2 to attack rolls, saving throws, skill checks, and ability checks.
Physically ill. −2 to attack rolls, weapon damage, saving throws, skill checks, and ability checks.
Unconscious; can be awoken by noise or damage. Treated as helpless.
Can only take a single move or standard action each round (not both, no full-round actions).
Drops everything held, cannot take actions, −2 to AC, loses Dex bonus to AC.
Knocked out. Treated as helpless. Unaware of surroundings.
Loses hit points each round equal to the bleed value. Healed by any cure effect or a DC 15 Heal check.
An item with this condition is damaged and functions at half effectiveness. Weapons deal −2 damage and have a 50% chance to misfire (firearms).
Affected by a magical curse. Penalties vary by curse; removal typically requires remove curse or break enchantment.
Suffering from a disease. Must succeed at periodic Fortitude saves or suffer ability damage/drain until cured.
Affected by a poison. Must make Fortitude saves at intervals to avoid ability damage or other effects until cured or the poison runs its course.
Permanently lacks functional eyes, not merely blinded by an effect. Same mechanical penalties as blinded.
A free melee attack provoked when a foe in your threatened area takes a distracting action (casting, moving out, etc.).
An attack bonus derived from class and level. Added to attack rolls; governs iterative attacks and combat maneuver checks.
Combat maneuver to push an opponent straight back without dealing damage.
Move twice your speed in a straight line and make one melee attack at +2 to hit; −2 AC until next turn.
Bonus for special combat maneuvers. CMB = BAB + Str modifier + special size modifier.
Defense against special combat maneuvers. CMD = 10 + BAB + Str + Dex + special size modifier.
A full-round action to deliver a killing blow to a helpless creature. On a hit, it deals damage and the target must save or die.
A confirmed attack in the threat range of a weapon multiplies damage by the weapon's critical multiplier.
Combat maneuver to knock a weapon from a foe's hand. Uses CMB vs. CMD.
Bluff check (DC 10 + BAB + Wis mod) as a standard action to deny a foe their Dex bonus to AC until your next attack.
Two allies threatening an opponent from opposite sides each gain +2 to attack rolls against it.
A full-round action allowing all iterative attacks from BAB (at −5 per attack beyond the first).
Combat maneuver to move through an opponent's square. Success moves you through; failure stops you.
Rogue ability granting extra damage dice when attacking a flat-footed or flanked target.
Combat maneuver to damage or destroy a held or worn item on an opponent.
A standard action that grants +4 dodge bonus to AC for one round; you cannot attack.
Combat maneuver to knock an opponent prone. If failed by 10+, you fall prone instead.
Fighting with a weapon in each hand imposes attack penalties (−6/−10 reduced by feats and light offhand).
A standard action; make a DC 10 attack roll to grant an ally +2 to their next attack roll or AC.
Partial or total obscurement (fog, darkness, etc.) grants the target a miss chance: 20% for partial, 50% for total concealment.
Physical obstruction between attacker and target. Grants +4 to AC and +2 to Reflex saves; soft cover only +4 AC.
Combat maneuver to inflict a condition (blinded, entangled, sickened, etc.) on an opponent for 1 round.
Combat maneuver to pull a creature directly toward you; creature moves into the space between you.
Take −4 to attack rolls to gain +2 dodge bonus to AC for the round (improved by Combat Expertise).
Combat maneuver to move an opponent to a different square within your reach without dealing damage.
Combat maneuver to take an unattended or loosely held item from an opponent using CMB vs. CMD.
A full-round action to move up to double speed without provoking an attack of opportunity from the first square left.
A touch attack made at range. Ignores armor and natural armor bonuses but is subject to range penalties and cover.
The squares a creature can make attacks of opportunity into — typically all squares within reach.
The distance at which a creature (or weapon) can threaten and attack. Most Medium creatures have 5-ft. reach; reach weapons extend to 10 ft.
Combat maneuver to seize and restrain an opponent. Success inflicts the grappled condition on both parties; continued success can pin the target.
The most common action type. Includes most attacks, casting a spell, and many special abilities. One per round.
An action that lets you move up to your speed or perform a comparably quick task (draw weapon, pick up item). One per round.
Consumes both your standard and move actions. Examples: full attack, run, casting a 1-round spell.
A very fast action consuming very little time. Only one swift action per turn; used for quickened spells and certain class abilities.
Like a swift action but can be taken even outside your turn. Using one means you lose your swift action on your next turn.
Negligibly quick actions (drop an item, speak a sentence). Several per round; GM may set reasonable limits.
Use a standard action to declare a trigger; when triggered, you act before or after that event (your choice at readying).
Voluntarily act later in the round. Your initiative count changes to the point you choose to act.
Move exactly 5 ft. without provoking attacks of opportunity. Can only be taken when you have not moved this turn.
Spell school specializing in protective and warding magic: shields, counterspells, banishment, and dispelling effects.
Spell school that brings creatures, objects, or materials from elsewhere — summons, teleportation, and healing fall here.
Spell school dedicated to revealing information: scrying, detection, true seeing, and knowledge-granting effects.
Spell school that affects minds and emotions. Includes charm and compulsion effects; most require the target to be a living creature.
Spell school that manipulates energy to produce destructive or impressive effects — fireballs, lightning bolts, and force effects.
Spell school creating false sensory impressions. Subtypes include figments, glamers, phantasms, patterns, and shadows.
Spell school manipulating life force and undeath. Includes energy drain, death effects, and animating corpses.
Spell school that physically alters creatures, objects, or conditions. Includes polymorph, enhancement, and movement effects.
Spells not belonging to any school; no specialist can forbid them and no specialist gains bonuses against them.
Energy type dealing corrosive damage. Not blocked by fire or cold resistance; no common immunity except from certain outsiders.
Energy type dealing freezing damage. Cold resistance or immunity negates or reduces it.
Energy type dealing lightning damage. Electricity resistance or immunity negates or reduces it.
Energy type dealing burning damage. Fire resistance or immunity negates or reduces it; vulnerability doubles it.
Energy type dealing damage via sound waves. Deafened creatures are not immune; rare resistance exists on some creatures.
A magical ability that works like a spell but requires no components. Provokes AoOs, subject to SR, and requires concentration checks.
A magical ability that does not provoke AoOs, is not subject to SR, and cannot be counterspelled. It can be suppressed in antimagic.
A non-magical special ability. Works in antimagic, cannot be dispelled, and is not subject to SR.
Remove or suppress an ongoing spell or magical effect. Dispel check = d20 + caster level vs. DC 11 + effect's caster level.
Interrupt an opponent's spellcasting with a readied action. Must cast the same spell or a counterspell substitute (e.g., dispel magic).
Feats that modify a spell's power at the cost of increasing its effective spell level. Examples: Empower, Maximize, Quicken, Silent.
The number a target must roll (d20 + save bonus) to resist a spell or effect. DC = 10 + spell level + casting stat modifier.
A spell requiring specific spoken words. Cannot be cast while deafened (20% failure) or silenced.
A spell requiring specific hand gestures. Requires a free hand; wearing armor may cause arcane spell failure.
A physical item consumed or used in spellcasting. Usually trivially cheap; stored in a spell component pouch.
A reusable physical object used in spellcasting; not consumed. Different from a material component.
A tag on a spell (fear, mind-affecting, light, fire, etc.) that indicates additional properties used by game mechanics and creature immunities.
Spells cast by bards, sorcerers, wizards, and other arcane classes. Subject to arcane spell failure from armor.
Spells drawn from a deity or natural power, cast by clerics, druids, paladins, and rangers. Not subject to arcane spell failure.
A third type of magic used by psychics and related classes. Uses thought and emotion components instead of verbal/somatic.
A number from 0 to 9 indicating a spell's power. Determines slot usage, save DCs, and whether a caster can access it.
A prepared caster's daily resource for casting spells; each spell prepared or spell spontaneously cast expends one slot of the appropriate level.
0-level spells. Arcane casters (wizards/sorcerers) can cast them unlimited times; some divine casters call them orisons.
A 0-level divine spell. Clerics, druids, and similar casters prepare orisons and can cast them at will.
Reduces damage from physical attacks by a set number, bypassed by specified materials or alignments (e.g., DR 5/silver).
Reduces damage of a specific energy type (fire, cold, etc.) by a set amount per attack.
Regains a set number of hit points at the start of each turn automatically.
An object's resistance to damage; each attack's damage is reduced by the hardness value.
Like fast healing, but lethal damage becomes nonlethal. Damage from specified sources (e.g., fire) still kills.
Complete protection against a specific damage type, condition, or effect. No damage or effect is taken regardless of magnitude.
A weakness to a specific damage type; the creature takes 150% damage (half again as much) from that source.
Can see in total darkness out to a specified range, but only in black and white.
Can see twice as far as a human in dim light. Elves, gnomes, and many other races share this ability.
Detects creatures within range without sight; the creature is still aware of a foe's presence but does not ignore concealment.
Perceives the environment precisely without relying on vision, negating concealment and miss chances up to its listed range.
Detects creatures by smell up to 30 ft. (60 ft. downwind, 15 ft. upwind). Can pinpoint a foe within 5 ft.
Automatically senses creatures touching the same surface (ground, water) within the listed range.
A creature's ability to shrug off spells. Caster must beat SR with a d20 + caster level roll for a spell to affect it.
A percentage chance (rolled on d100) that a physically valid attack still misses — granted by concealment, blur, displacement, etc.
Temporary loss of ability score points. Healed by rest (1 point/day) or magic.
Permanent loss of ability score points. Requires restoration magic to recover.
Percentage chance an arcane spell fails when the caster wears armor that interferes with somatic components.
A measure of magical power, usually equal to class level. Affects spell range, duration, and damage caps.
A check to cast or maintain a spell while distracted. DC varies by distraction type; key ability modifier applies.
Cha-based skill used to change attitudes and request favors. Attitude shifts take 1 minute of conversation.
Saving throw based on Constitution. Resists physical threats: poison, disease, death effects.
Dice used to determine hit points gained per level. Also used to measure creature power for spell effects.
A d20 + Dex modifier roll at the start of combat that determines the order of action.
Additional attacks granted at BAB +6, +11, and +16, each made at −5 from the previous.
Saving throw based on Dexterity. Resists area effects and dodgeable dangers.
A die roll to resist or reduce a harmful effect. Three types: Fortitude (Con), Reflex (Dex), Will (Wis).
An attack that only requires touching the target; ignores natural and armor bonuses to AC (not Dex or shield).
Saving throw based on Wisdom. Resists mental and mind-affecting effects, fear, compulsion, and illusions.
A descriptor of a creature's moral and ethical disposition on two axes: Good/Neutral/Evil and Lawful/Neutral/Chaotic.
A number indicating how difficult a monster is. A party of four at that level should spend about 25% of daily resources defeating it.
Creatures range from Fine to Colossal (Fine, Diminutive, Tiny, Small, Medium, Large, Huge, Gargantuan, Colossal). Size affects attack, AC, CMB, CMD, and stealth.
An AC bonus representing tough hide or scales. Stacks with armor but not with other natural armor bonuses.
Damage intended to knock out rather than kill. When nonlethal equals or exceeds current HP, the creature is staggered, then unconscious.
A dying creature that has stopped losing hit points. A stable creature is still unconscious but no longer makes Con checks.
The ability (via Channel Energy for clerics) to damage or destroy undead in a radius. Positive energy harms undead; negative energy harms living creatures.
The system governing how many and what kinds of actions a creature can take in a round (standard, move, swift, free, immediate).
Bonuses of the same type (e.g., enhancement, morale, luck) do not stack — only the highest applies. Dodge bonuses and untyped bonuses are exceptions.
The target number an attacker must meet or exceed to hit a creature. AC = 10 + armor + shield + Dex + size + other bonuses.
A measure of a creature's health. Damage reduces HP; at 0 the creature is disabled; below 0 they are dying; at −Con they die.
Points awarded for overcoming challenges. Accumulating XP leads to gaining levels according to the campaign's advancement track.
A square that a creature with a melee weapon can immediately attack. Enemies moving out of these squares provoke attacks of opportunity.
The six core statistics: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. Each generates a modifier of (score − 10) / 2.
Points invested in a skill (max = character level). Each rank grants +1 to that skill check; cross-class skills cost 1 rank each.
A skill on a class's list. Having at least 1 rank in a class skill grants +3 to checks with it.
An advanced class that requires meeting specific prerequisites before entry. Provides specialized abilities unavailable to base classes.
A variant of a base class that swaps out certain class features for different abilities, allowing customized specialization.
Each character designates one class as their favored class. Gaining a level in it grants +1 HP or +1 skill rank, or a special racial bonus.
Taking levels in more than one class. BAB, saves, and spells each advance separately per class.
